growth_impulse_drilldown.intro: Growth Impulses asks whether global growth/risk appetite is accelerating or fading.
growth_impulse_drilldown.sort_note: Rows sort by absolute current z-score (|current z|), so the strongest positive or negative signals rise first; 7-day contribution is still proxied as each input's 7-day fast z-score change divided by the 10 growth-impulse components.

rows.label: BDRY/BDI proxy
rows.explanation: Dry-bulk shipping proxy; rising values can indicate stronger real-economy trade and commodity demand.

rows.label: XLI/XLU
rows.explanation: Industrials versus utilities; a higher ratio signals cyclicals beating defensives.

rows.label: SPHB/SPLV
rows.explanation: High-beta equities versus low-volatility equities; a higher ratio signals investors favor riskier stocks.

rows.label: WEI
rows.explanation: Weekly Economic Index; high, rising, or falling readings capture broad US economic activity momentum.

rows.label: HYG
rows.explanation: High-yield bond ETF; rising HYG usually signals stronger risk appetite and credit conditions.

rows.label: XLY/XLP
rows.explanation: Consumer discretionary versus staples; a higher ratio signals cyclical consumer risk appetite.

rows.label: Copper
rows.explanation: Cyclical commodity proxy for industrial and global growth demand.

rows.label: VIX inverted
rows.explanation: Equity volatility, inverted so lower volatility is positive for risk appetite.

rows.label: HY spread inverted
rows.explanation: High-yield credit spread, inverted so tighter spreads are positive for growth and risk appetite.

rows.label: Yield curve
rows.explanation: 10Y minus 2Y Treasury yield; a steeper curve usually supports growth and liquidity expectations.

brief: Growth Impulses improved over the latest week, led by XLI/XLU (+0.15 contribution points).
At +0.12, GII is currently supporting MMI.
Support currently comes from XLI/XLU (+1.49z) and SPHB/SPLV (+1.03z).
The main drag is BDRY/BDI proxy (-1.53z) and WEI (-0.48z).
Input breadth is mixed: 5 of 8 inputs are positive and 3 inputs are negative.
The next read improves if BDRY/BDI proxy stops acting as a drag and more inputs join the positive side; it weakens if XLI/XLU rolls over.

sector_breadth_drilldown.intro: Sector Breadth asks whether cyclical equity participation is broadening or narrowing.
sector_breadth_drilldown.sort_note: Rows sort by absolute current z-score (|current z|), so the strongest positive or negative Breadth signals rise first; 7-day contribution is still proxied as each input's 7-day z-score change divided by the 7 sector breadth components.

rows.label: XRT
rows.explanation: Retail reflects consumer-facing cyclical participation beyond defensive staples.

rows.label: XHB
rows.explanation: Homebuilders are rate-sensitive cyclicals and often react early to financial conditions.

rows.label: IYT
rows.explanation: Transports track economically sensitive shipping, logistics, and travel activity.

rows.label: KBE
rows.explanation: Banks capture credit-cycle health, yield-curve sensitivity, and domestic risk appetite.

rows.label: SMH
rows.explanation: Semiconductors are a high-beta cyclical leadership group; strength broadens risk appetite.

rows.label: IWM
rows.explanation: Small caps reflect domestic cyclical participation and financing sensitivity.

rows.label: IBB
rows.explanation: Biotech adds speculative growth participation outside mega-cap technology.

brief: Sector Breadth improved over the latest week, led by IBB (+0.23 contribution points).
At +1.60, Breadth is currently supporting MMI.
Support currently comes from XRT (+1.85z) and XHB (+1.79z).
Input breadth is broadly constructive: 7 of 7 inputs are positive and 0 inputs are negative.
The next read improves if IBB stops acting as a drag and more inputs join the positive side; it weakens if XRT rolls over.

financial_conditions_drilldown.intro: Financial Conditions asks whether volatility and credit stress are loosening or tightening.
financial_conditions_drilldown.sort_note: Rows sort by absolute current z-score (|current z|), so the strongest positive or negative FinCon signals rise first; 7-day contribution is still proxied as each input's 7-day z-score change divided by the 3 financial conditions components.

rows.label: VIX
rows.explanation: S&P 500 implied volatility. The z-score is inverted, so falling VIX loosens financial conditions and supports MMI.

rows.label: MOVE
rows.explanation: Treasury-market implied volatility. The z-score is inverted, so calmer rate volatility is positive for financial conditions.

rows.label: HY spread
rows.explanation: High-yield option-adjusted spread. The z-score is inverted, so tighter credit spreads read as looser conditions.

brief: Financial Conditions improved over the latest week, led by VIX (+0.52 contribution points).
At +0.49, FinCon is currently supporting MMI.
Support currently comes from VIX (+0.70z) and MOVE (+0.28z).
Input breadth is broadly constructive: 2 of 2 inputs are positive and 0 inputs are negative.
The next read improves if MOVE stops acting as a drag and more inputs join the positive side; it weakens if VIX rolls over.
